notes: Year 2000 will appear as '00' to many systems, interpreted as 1900

metric_value: Crash buying sprees mid-1997 through mid-1999; professional Y2K service fees to increase 1997-1999 due to staff scarcity

metric_value: Non-Y2K-safe businesses will lose competitive ground to Y2K-ready competitors; some corporate viability at risk

metric_value: Global Y2K spending estimated $300-600 billion; U.S. federal spending ~$7.5B; corporate ~$134B in U.S.
notes: Spending surge prediction proved correct. Source: Library of Congress Y2K resource (https://guides.loc.gov/this-month-in-business-history/january/y2k); Wikipedia Y2K article (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Year_2000_problem).

metric_value: Contrary to fears, few major errors occurred in 2000; limited outages attributed to pre-emptive remediation spending
notes: Clinton labeled Y2K 'first challenge of 21st century successfully met'. Source: Wikipedia Y2K (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Year_2000_problem).

metric_value: Major retailers including Wal-Mart did enforce Y2K compliance requirements on suppliers; supply chain compliance became industry standard
notes: Aberdeen's Wal-Mart prediction proved directionally correct.

metric_name: Aberdeen best practices for Y2K executive response

notes: 4-step best practice framework consistent with Aberdeen's IBM study observations
